div1.entryFree:
Bench, subst. 1) a long seat: Tw. I, 5, 158. H4A I, 2, 4. Rom. II, 4, 37. Caes. III, 2, 263.
2) the seat of judges, of senators: to pluck down justice from your awful b. H4B V, 2, 86. pluck the grave wrinkled senate from the b. Tim. IV, 1, 5. senators on the b. IV, 3, 37.
3) the senate itself: a graver b. than ever frowned in Greece, Cor. III, 1, 106. their obedience fails to the greater b. Cor. III, 1, 106
